title: Full Latin print model (A–Z, a–z, 0–9) + SOURCES.md
context: Building on the 5-glyph pilot, the full Latin print model extends coverage to all 62 glyphs (A–Z, a–z, 0–9) required for complete early-literacy practice sheets. Every glyph must have a per-glyph sourceRef citing an authoritative formation source, outlines from the gate-verified open font, and reviewer sign-off. National-style variants (e.g. single vs. double-storey 'a'/'g', open vs. closed '4') must be labeled with their source/region rather than presented as uniquely correct — the project is non-prescriptive.
objective: Model all 62 Latin print glyphs (A–Z, a–z, 0–9) as schema-valid GlyphModel JSON with per-glyph sourceRef, commit SOURCES.md recording every formation source and the font provenance with snapshots, and obtain formation reviewer sign-off on the complete per-glyph checklist.
acceptanceCriteria: All 62 glyphs (A–Z uppercase, a–z lowercase, 0–9 digits) modeled as schema-valid GlyphModel JSON with ordered strokes[], guidelineAlignment, startPoint, directionHint, and a required sourceRef per glyph.
SOURCES.md committed recording every formation source (name, URL, license, retrieval date, snapshot SHA-256 + Wayback URL) and the font provenance; provenance-gate green across the full set.
National-style variants (e.g. single/double-storey a/g, open/closed 4) are modeled as labeled StyleVariants citing their source/region; no variant is presented as the single correct form.
Formation reviewer signs the per-glyph checklist for all 62 glyphs confirming stroke order and direction match the cited source.
resources: PLAN.md section 9 M1, section 7 (licensing), section 16 Q6 (national-style neutrality)
output: data/latin/ directory with 62 schema-valid GlyphModel JSON files, SOURCES.md with complete provenance records and snapshots, and a reviewer-signed per-glyph checklist — all committed CC-BY-4.0.
